reasoning: The extraction contains a critical programmatic defect where header and footer artifacts were retained across almost every page of the document (35 instances over 18 pages), which will cause severe repetition for screen readers. Additionally, there are multiple major defects including a broken heading hierarchy, incomplete alternative text for 10 images, and a table caption improperly coded as a header row. Per the count-based routing rules, any critical defect mandates a REJECT.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (35): 2023 Roanoke Rapids Vegetation Survey Report; 1; 2023 Roanoke Rapids Vegetation Survey Report
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=35, pages_affected=18/19. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

The output fails to maintain the correct structural containment relationship for major sections. In the source document, 'Conclusions and Management Implications' and 'References' are formatted with bold and underlined text, identical to top-level sections like 'Methods' and 'Results and Discussion'. However, in the JSON output, these sections are assigned as level 4 headings (H4). Because they immediately follow 'Coontail' (which was assigned H3), this incorrect level assignment structurally ne
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Top-level section heading styled identically to previous top-level headings: 'Conclusions and Management Implications'

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 1 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=1. Examples: p6: "Roanoke Rapids". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 10 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (10 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...y with wispy clouds. A rocky, muddy shoreline with"; p1: "...in white uppercase letters. The words NC STATE are"; p7: "...nd brownish aquatic plants growing thickly beneath"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=10 (truncated=10, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The text 'Prepared By:' and 'Submitted To:' on the first page of the source document are underlined (and italicized). In the extraction output, these phrases are marked with italics but the underline emphasis has been entirely lost.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['https://www.dominionenergy.com/projects-and-facilities/hydro']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=1. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Configure extraction to properly filter out repeating headers and footers.
Ensure complete and descriptive alternative text is generated for all images.
Fix heading hierarchy to reflect major sections accurately (e.g., 'Conclusions' and 'References' should be H1 or H2, not H4).
Ensure table captions are coded using the <caption> tag rather than spanning header rows.
